title: First Non-Repeating Character
description:
Find the first non-repeating character in a string.

Return the character itself. If none exists, return a space (' ').

visibleTestCases.explanation: l is the first non-repeating character

hiddenTestCases.explanation: w is the first non-repeating character
u is the first non-repeating character

def firstUniqueChar(s):
    # TODO: Find first non-repeating character
    return ' '

import sys
def main():
    line = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
    if line.startswith('"') and line.endswith('"'):
        line = line[1:-1]
    res = firstUniqueChar(line)
    print(f'"{res}"')
if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
